---
title: "Bingjin Xue"
page-layout: full
pagetitle: "Bingjin Xue — Economist"
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <meta name="description" content="Bingjin Xue — Assistant Professor of Economics at the University of New Hampshire. Research in health economics, applied microeconometrics, and causal inference.">
---

::: {.home-hero}

![](assets/photo.jpg)

<div class="bio">

# Bingjin Xue

<div class="affiliation">Assistant Professor of Economics · Paul College of Business and Economics · University of New Hampshire</div>

<p>
Bingjin Xue is an Assistant Professor of Economics at the University of New Hampshire. His research uses quasi-experimental methods to study how policies and institutions shape health, labor, and economic behavior. Current work examines how post-Dobbs abortion restrictions affect economic hardship and crime, how naloxone access laws spill over into reproductive health, how e-cigarette regulations change birth outcomes, and how electronic health record transitions reshape physician referral networks.
</p>

<p>
His work has appeared in the *Journal of Health Economics* and the *Journal of Development Economics*. He received his PhD in Business and Economics from Lehigh University in 2023. He teaches undergraduate microeconomics and graduate courses on predictive modeling and machine learning for economists.
</p>

<div class="links">
[Email](mailto:bingjin.xue@unh.edu) ·
[CV](assets/CV.pdf) ·
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOUR_ID) ·
[GitHub](https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME) ·
[SSRN](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=YOUR_ID)
</div>

</div>
:::
